public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as permit clerk.
appearance: Nyelle Starbloom carries the marks of permit clerk in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a permit clerk still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of permit clerk in and around Verdant Row District, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Verdant Row District while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Nyelle Starbloom can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.
Nyelle Starbloom can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Nyelle Starbloom communicates in public. In Verdant Row District, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Fashion Artisans Collective, Roadwarden command staff.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Verdant Row District: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague

first_impression: Nyelle Starbloom presents as Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In Shelk, that reads as someone polished, socially alert, and attentive to harmony, status, and cultural presentation, and the habits of a permit clerk still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Permit Clerk, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Shelk.
what_they_refuse: Nyelle Starbloom refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Permit Clerk too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Nyelle Starbloom can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Nyelle Starbloom adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Nyelle Starbloom is Known locally through day-to-day work as permit clerk. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Permit Clerk.
social_texture: Nyelle Starbloom speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Verdant Row District, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Nyelle touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Nyelle Starbloom when the party needs a socially grounded face for Verdant Row District: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
